title: [cross-impl] Audit SCHC Python vs C rule application, CDA bit ordering, and fragmentation state machine
description: Python (python/src/lichen/schc/*.py) and C (lichen/subsys/lichen/schc/schc.c + link integration) both target same pre-provisioned rules and test/vectors/schc*.json but lack explicit cross-language byte-for-byte interop harness. Potential subtle differences in: rule priority/selection, CDA (LSB/MSB on variable fields), residue padding/alignment, fragmentation RCS computation, ACK bitmap handling, error paths, L2 dispatch in l2_payload.py vs frame.c. Add interop test (Python drives C via FFI or runs both and compares) or expand Zephyr tests to assert exact match on all vectors. Critical for Phase 2 SCHC consistency.
